 {"id":"bd-28cpd","title":"PromptInjector: Handle Codex double-Enter quirk","description":"## Purpose
Handle Codex-specific quirk that requires double-Enter to submit prompts, ensuring reliable prompt injection across all agent types.

## Implementation Details

```go
// AgentQuirks defines agent-specific prompt submission behaviors
type AgentQuirks struct {
    DoubleEnter    bool   // Codex requires double Enter
    EscapePrefix   bool   // Some agents need Escape before input
    ClearLineFirst bool   // Clear existing input before prompt
}

var agentQuirksMap = map[string]AgentQuirks{
    "cod": {DoubleEnter: true},
    "cc":  {DoubleEnter: false},
    "gmi": {DoubleEnter: false},
}

// SendWithQuirks sends prompt handling agent-specific requirements
func (pi *PromptInjector) SendWithQuirks(paneID, agentType, prompt string) error {
    quirks := agentQuirksMap[agentType]
    
    pi.logger.Debug("applying agent quirks",
        "pane", paneID,
        "agent_type", agentType,
        "double_enter", quirks.DoubleEnter)
    
    // Clear line if needed
    if quirks.ClearLineFirst {
        if err := pi.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, "C-u", false); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("clear line: %w", err)
        }
    }
    
    // Send the prompt text
    if err := pi.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, prompt, true); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("send prompt: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Handle Codex double-Enter quirk
    if quirks.DoubleEnter {
        pi.logger.Debug("sending extra Enter for Codex", "pane", paneID)
        time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond) // Brief pause between Enters
        if err := pi.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, "", true); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("send second enter: %w", err)
        }
    }
    
    pi.logger.Info("prompt sent with quirks applied",
        "pane", paneID,
        "agent_type", agentType)
    return nil
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log which quirks are being applied per agent type
- Debug-level log for double-Enter sends
- Info-level confirmation on successful send

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Codex prompts reliably submitted with double-Enter
- [ ] Quirks map is extensible for future agent types
- [ ] Small delay between Enters to ensure reliable submission
- [ ] No quirks applied to cc/gmi agents
- [ ] Unit tests verify quirk application per agent type","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:39.308715362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:40:03.952997406Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:40:03.952294280Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28cpd","depends_on_id":"bd-17zih","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:18:55.075460619Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-28cpd","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:19:18.350206855Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28n0d","title":"Implement shell environment detection","description":"Implement shell environment detection.

## What

Detect shell type and common configuration that affects agent operation.

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "shell": {
    "type": "zsh",              // bash, zsh, fish, etc.
    "tmux_plugin_detected": true,  // May cause issues
    "oh_my_zsh_detected": true,    // Common zsh framework
    "config_path": "~/.zshrc"    // Where to look for aliases
  }
}
```

## Detection Methods

### Shell Type
- Read $SHELL environment variable
- Verify against running shell process

### tmux Plugin Detection
Check common plugin locations:
- ~/.oh-my-zsh/plugins/tmux
- ~/.zshrc contains 'plugins=(... tmux ...)'
- ~/.tmux.conf sourced plugins

### oh-my-zsh Detection
- Check for ~/.oh-my-zsh directory
- Check ZSH environment variable

## Why This Matters

tmux plugins can:
- Alias the tmux command
- Add automatic session attachment
- Modify key bindings

Agents need to know to use /usr/bin/tmux directly when plugins detected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:40:42.601068157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:17.787668899Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:17.787582285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28n0d","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:40:42.629141697Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28o9m","title":"Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for listing reasoning modes","description":"# Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for listing/browsing reasoning modes

## Command Structure
```
ntm ensemble modes [subcommand] [flags]

Subcommands:
  list    List modes (default)
  show    Show full details for a single mode
```

## List Command
```
ntm ensemble modes list [flags]
  --category string   Filter by category (e.g., Formal, Causal)
  --tier string       Filter by tier (core|advanced|experimental)
  --all               Alias for --tier all (show all tiers)
  --format string     Output format: table, json, yaml (default: table)
  --verbose           Show full descriptions + failure modes
```

### Default Behavior
- **Default list = core tier only** (curated).
- If advanced modes exist, print a hint: `Use --all to show advanced modes`.

## Show Command
```
ntm ensemble modes show <mode-id|code> [flags]
  --format string     Output format: text, json, yaml (default: text)
```

## Behavior
- `ntm ensemble modes` with no subcommand defaults to `list`.
- Accepts lookup by **mode id** (lowercase) and **mode code** (e.g., A1).
- JSON/YAML outputs include: id, code, tier, name, category, short_desc, outputs, best_for, failure_modes, differentiator, icon, color.

## File Location
internal/cli/ensemble_modes.go

## Dependencies
- Requires mode catalog loader (bd-qn9gc)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default list shows only core tier
- [ ] Category + tier filters work
- [ ] Show displays full mode details
- [ ] JSON/YAML outputs include code + tier
- [ ] `modes` defaults to `list`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:23.818764532Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:53:42.069282507Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:53:42.069258051Z","close_reason":"Implemented as 'ntm modes' command (not 'ntm ensemble modes').  {"id":"bd-29kr","title":"Add session profiles - save spawn config + prompts as named profile for quick reuse","description":"Add session profiles - save spawn config + prompts as named profile for quick reuse.

## Problem Being Solved

Users frequently spawn sessions with same configuration. Profiles allow saving and reusing spawn configs.

## Command Design

### Save Profile
```bash
ntm profile save PROFILE_NAME [--from SESSION]
ntm profile save myproject --cc=3 --cod=2 --prompt-file init.md
```

### Use Profile
```bash
ntm spawn SESSION --profile PROFILE_NAME
ntm spawn daily_work --profile myproject
```

### List Profiles
```bash
ntm profile list
```

### Delete Profile
```bash
ntm profile delete PROFILE_NAME
```

## Profile Storage
~/.config/ntm/profiles/PROFILE_NAME.toml

```toml
[spawn]
cc = 3
cod = 2
gmi = 0

[prompts]
init_prompt_file = "init.md"

[options]
auto_accept = true
```

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-profile-list
ntm --robot-profile-show=PROFILE_NAME

## Acceptance Criteria

1. ntm profile save creates profile file in ~/.config/ntm/profiles/
2. ntm profile list shows all saved profiles
3. ntm profile delete removes profile
4. ntm spawn --profile loads profile and uses config
5. Profile config overridden by explicit flags
6. Robot mode returns JSON for list/show
7. Profile file uses TOML format

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/profile_test.go
- Test save/load/delete operations
- Test profile merging with flags

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-PROFILES] Session Profiles Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-PROFILES]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing session profiles"
ntm profile save test_profile --cc=2 --cod=1
ntm profile list | grep test_profile || exit 1
ntm profile delete test_profile
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Profiles test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Session profiles save spawn config
2. Profiles include agent counts and prompts
3. Named profiles stored in config directory
4. ntm spawn --profile NAME loads profile
5. ntm profile list shows available profiles
6. ntm profile save NAME creates profile
7. Unit tests for profile serialization
8. E2E test for profile creation and loading","notes":"## Testing Requirements

- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.

 {"id":"bd-49fb9","title":"EPIC: Reasoning Ensemble Mode - Multi-agent cognitive diversity system","description":"# Reasoning Ensemble Mode - EPIC

A multi-agent cognitive diversity system that spawns parallel agents, each using a distinct reasoning lens, then synthesizes their structured outputs into actionable insights.

## Core Philosophy

**Users pick goals, not modes.** Ensembles are the primary interface; modes are implementation details.

- Taxonomy source: **all 80 modes** in `modes_of_reasoning.md`
- **V1 ships full 80-mode catalog** with tiered visibility:
  - **core** (curated default set, ~28)
  - **advanced** (full catalog, hidden by default)
  - **experimental** (optional future tier)
- Default UX shows only **core** modes; advanced modes are accessible via explicit flags (no feature loss).
- Mode metadata includes `code` (A1, B3, ...) and `tier` to power filtering + warnings.

## Named Ensembles (Primary Interface)

1. **Project Diagnosis** - systems-thinking + adversarial review + dependency mapping + failure-mode + perspective-taking
2. **Idea Forge** - conceptual blending + analogical + design-space + second-order-effects + option-generation
3. **Spec Critique** - deductive + ambiguity-detection + edge-case + test-plan + perspective-taking
4. **Safety / Risk** - worst-case + adversarial review + compliance + failure-mode + root-cause
5. **Architecture Review** - argument mapping + root-cause + systems-thinking + perspective-taking + strategic-planning
6. **Tech Debt Triage** - dependency-mapping + failure-mode + resource-allocation + prioritization
7. **Bug Hunt** - diagnostic + abductive + adversarial + deductive + mechanistic + type-theoretic
8. **Root Cause** - diagnostic + causal + counterfactual + mechanistic + abductive + debiasing
9. **Strategic Planning** - strategic-planning + systems-thinking + decision-under-uncertainty + second-order-effects + resource-allocation + prioritization + perspective-taking

## 3-Stage Pipeline

### Stage 1: Intake / Context Pack
- Auto-generate project brief (README, structure, recent commits, beads)
- Ask targeted questions if context is thin
- Cache context pack for reuse

### Stage 2: Mode Run (Parallel)
Each agent receives:
- Base instructions (tone, constraints)
- Mode preamble (the reasoning lens)
- **Output schema contract** (mandatory structured YAML)

### Stage 3: Synthesis / Arbitration
- **Synthesizer agent**: merges outputs into cohesive report
- **Disagreement auditor**: highlights conflicts, evidence needed, which modes disagree

## Structured Output Schema (Critical)

Every mode MUST output the same fields for mechanical merging. (The system will **inject `mode_id`** if missing but warns.)

```yaml
mode_id: string             # Optional in agent output; injected if missing
thesis: string              # One-sentence main insight
confidence: 0.0-1.0         # Overall confidence

top_findings:
  - finding: string
    impact: critical|high|medium|low
    confidence: 0.0-1.0
    evidence_pointer: string  # file:line or artifact reference
    reasoning: string         # optional

risks:
  - risk: string
    impact: critical|high|medium|low
    likelihood: 0.0-1.0       # string aliases high|medium|low accepted
    mitigation: string
    affected_areas: [string]

recommendations:
  - recommendation: string
    priority: critical|high|medium|low
    effort: low|medium|high
    rationale: string
    related_findings: [int]

questions_for_user:
  - question: string
    context: string
    blocking: bool
    suggested_answers: [string]

failure_modes_to_watch:
  - mode: string
    description: string
    indicators: [string]
    prevention: string
```

## Mode Catalog & Preambles

- **All 80 modes are shipped** in the embedded catalog with full metadata.
- **Core (curated) modes** have hand-authored preambles.
- **Advanced modes** use a fallback template that renders from metadata (description, outputs, best_for, failure_modes) so they are usable without bespoke files.
- CLI/robot listings default to core; `--all` / `--tier` shows advanced.

## Budget & Early Stopping (Critical)

Swarm features can become token black holes. Mandatory controls:
- **Per-agent token caps**
- **Marginal utility stopping**
- **Context pack caching**
- **Budget dashboard**

## Observability Metrics

- **Coverage map**: which reasoning categories were invoked
- **Redundancy score**: similarity between outputs (high = wasteful)
- **Findings velocity**: new insights per token spent
- **Conflict density**: disagreements per output pair

## Implementation Phases

Phase 1 (P1): Core infrastructure
- Full 80-mode catalog + tiered filtering
- Intake system + ensemble orchestrator + budget controls
- Basic synthesis with disagreement detection

Phase 2 (P2): Polish
- Full TUI integration + metrics
- Robot API + advanced configuration
- Comprehensive unit + E2E tests with detailed logging

## Success Criteria

1. User can run `ntm ensemble project-diagnosis` and get actionable insights without mode knowledge
2. Outputs are schema-compliant and mechanically mergeable
3. Token spend is predictable and observable
4. Conflicts between modes are surfaced, not hidden
5.  {"id":"bd-4gaaz","title":"Task: Define YAML handoff format and types","description":"## Overview
Define the canonical YAML handoff format that will be used across NTM for context preservation.

## Background & Reasoning
From Continuous-Claude-v3: YAML handoffs are ~400 tokens vs ~2000 tokens for markdown. This 80% reduction matters because:
1. Less context overhead when loading handoffs
2. More room for actual work context
3. Parseable by both humans and machines
4. Status line integration requires consistent field names

## Deliverables

### 1. Create internal/handoff/types.go
```go
package handoff

import "time"

// Version tracks handoff format evolution for backwards compatibility
const HandoffVersion = "1.0"

type Handoff struct {
    // Metadata
    Version   string    `yaml:"version"`   // Format version for migrations
    Session   string    `yaml:"session"`
    Date      string    `yaml:"date"`
    CreatedAt time.Time `yaml:"created_at"` // Precise timestamp
    UpdatedAt time.Time `yaml:"updated_at"` // For edits

    // Status tracking
    Status   string `yaml:"status"`   // complete|partial|blocked
    Outcome  string `yaml:"outcome"`  // SUCCEEDED|PARTIAL_PLUS|PARTIAL_MINUS|FAILED

    // Core fields (REQUIRED for status line)
    Goal string `yaml:"goal"` // What this session accomplished - REQUIRED
    Now  string `yaml:"now"`  // What next session should do first - REQUIRED
    Test string `yaml:"test"` // Command to verify this work

    // Work tracking
    DoneThisSession []TaskRecord `yaml:"done_this_session"`

    // Context for future self
    Blockers  []string          `yaml:"blockers"`
    Questions []string          `yaml:"questions"`
    Decisions map[string]string `yaml:"decisions"`
    Findings  map[string]string `yaml:"findings"`

    // What worked and what didn't
    Worked []string `yaml:"worked"`
    Failed []string `yaml:"failed"`

    // Next steps
    Next []string `yaml:"next"`

    // File tracking
    Files FileChanges `yaml:"files"`

    // Integration fields - populated during recovery
    ActiveBeads      []string `yaml:"active_beads,omitempty"`       // From BV
    AgentMailThreads []string `yaml:"agent_mail_threads,omitempty"` // From Agent Mail
    CMMemories       []string `yaml:"cm_memories,omitempty"`        // From CM

    // Agent info for multi-agent sessions
    AgentID   string `yaml:"agent_id,omitempty"`
    AgentType string `yaml:"agent_type,omitempty"` // cc, cod, gmi
    PaneID    string `yaml:"pane_id,omitempty"`

    // Token context at time of handoff
    TokensUsed int     `yaml:"tokens_used,omitempty"`
    TokensMax  int     `yaml:"tokens_max,omitempty"`
    TokensPct  float64 `yaml:"tokens_pct,omitempty"`
}

type TaskRecord struct {
    Task  string   `yaml:"task"`
    Files []string `yaml:"files"`
}

type FileChanges struct {
    Created  []string `yaml:"created"`
    Modified []string `yaml:"modified"`
    Deleted  []string `yaml:"deleted"` // Track deletions too
}
```

### 2. Create internal/handoff/constants.go
```go
package handoff

const (
    StatusComplete = "complete"
    StatusPartial  = "partial"
    StatusBlocked  = "blocked"

    OutcomeSucceeded    = "SUCCEEDED"
    OutcomePartialPlus  = "PARTIAL_PLUS"
    OutcomePartialMinus = "PARTIAL_MINUS"
    OutcomeFailed       = "FAILED"
)

// Validation error types for detailed logging
type ValidationError struct {
    Field   string
    Message string
    Value   interface{}
}

func (e ValidationError) Error() string {
    return fmt.Sprintf("validation error: field=%s msg=%s value=%v", e.Field, e.Message, e.Value)
}
```

### 3. Create internal/handoff/validate.go
```go
package handoff

import (
    "fmt"
    "log/slog"
    "regexp"
    "time"
)

var (
    sessionNameRegex = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`)
    dateRegex        = regexp.MustCompile(`^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$`)
)

// Validate checks handoff for required fields and correct values
// Returns all validation errors (not just first) for comprehensive logging
func (h *Handoff) Validate() []ValidationError {
    var errs []ValidationError

    // Required fields
    if h.Goal == "" {
        errs = append(errs, ValidationError{
            Field:   "goal",
            Message: "required field missing - status line depends on this",
        })
    }
    if h.Now == "" {
        errs = append(errs, ValidationError{
            Field:   "now",
            Message: "required field missing - status line depends on this",
        })
    }

    // Session validation
    if h.Session != "" && h.Session != "general" {
        if !sessionNameRegex.MatchString(h.Session) {
            errs = append(errs, ValidationError{
                Field:   "session",
                Message: "must be alphanumeric with _ or - only",
                Value:   h.Session,
            })
        }
    }

    // Date format validation
    if h.Date != "" && !dateRegex.MatchString(h.Date) {
        errs = append(errs, ValidationError{
            Field:   "date",
            Message: "must be YYYY-MM-DD format",
            Value:   h.Date,
        })
    }

    // Status validation
    validStatuses := map[string]bool{
        StatusComplete: true, StatusPartial: true, StatusBlocked: true, "": true,
    }
    if !validStatuses[h.Status] {
        errs = append(errs, ValidationError{
            Field:   "status",
            Message: "must be complete, partial, or blocked",
            Value:   h.Status,
        })
    }

    // Outcome validation
    validOutcomes := map[string]bool{
        OutcomeSucceeded: true, OutcomePartialPlus: true,
        OutcomePartialMinus: true, OutcomeFailed: true, "": true,
    }
    if !validOutcomes[h.Outcome] {
        errs = append(errs, ValidationError{
            Field:   "outcome",
            Message: "must be SUCCEEDED, PARTIAL_PLUS, PARTIAL_MINUS, or FAILED",
            Value:   h.Outcome,
        })
    }

    // Log all validation errors at DEBUG level for troubleshooting
    for _, err := range errs {
        slog.Debug("handoff validation error",
            "field", err.Field,
            "message", err.Message,
            "value", err.Value,
            "session", h.Session,
        )
    }

    return errs
}

// IsValid returns true if handoff passes validation
func (h *Handoff) IsValid() bool {
    return len(h.Validate()) == 0
}

// SetDefaults populates default values for optional fields
func (h *Handoff) SetDefaults() {
    if h.Version == "" {
        h.Version = HandoffVersion
    }
    if h.Date == "" {
        h.Date = time.Now().Format("2006-01-02")
    }
    if h.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
        h.CreatedAt = time.Now()
    }
    h.UpdatedAt = time.Now()
}
```

### 4. Field Documentation
| Field | Required | Purpose |
|-------|----------|---------|
| version | auto | Format version for migrations |
| session | yes | Session identifier |
| date | auto | Date in YYYY-MM-DD |
| created_at | auto | Precise creation timestamp |
| goal | **YES** | What was accomplished - STATUS LINE |
| now | **YES** | What to do next - STATUS LINE |
| test | no | Verification command |
| status | no | complete/partial/blocked |
| outcome | no | SUCCEEDED/PARTIAL_PLUS/etc |
| done_this_session | no | Task records with files |
| blockers | no | Known blockers |
| decisions | no | Key decisions made |
| findings | no | Important discoveries |
| worked | no | What approaches worked |
| failed | no | What approaches failed |
| next | no | Suggested next steps |
| files | no | File change tracking |
| active_beads | no | BV integration |
| agent_mail_threads | no | Agent Mail integration |
| cm_memories | no | CM integration |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] YAML tags match exact field names from spec
- [ ] Constants for valid status/outcome values
- [ ] ValidationError type with structured fields
- [ ] Validate() returns ALL errors (not just first)
- [ ] Validation errors logged at DEBUG level with context
- [ ] SetDefaults() populates version, date, timestamps
- [ ] Integration fields (active_beads, etc.) defined with omitempty
- [ ] Unit tests for serialization/deserialization
- [ ] Unit tests for validation with various invalid inputs
- [ ] Example YAML file in testdata/

## Technical Notes
- Use gopkg.in/yaml.v3 for YAML parsing (already in go.mod)
- Keep field names exactly as specified - status line parser depends on `goal:` and `now:`
- DO NOT rename fields like `session_goal`, `objective`, `focus` - only `goal` and `now` work
- Use log/slog for structured logging
- Version field enables future format migrations without breaking old handoffs

- DEBUG: All validation errors with field, message, value, session
- DEBUG: SetDefaults called with resulting values
- INFO: Successful validation (for tracing handoff lifecycle)","notes":"Implemented YAML handoff format with types, validation, and comprehensive tests.  {"id":"bd-6wc0a","title":"TOON Integration: Token-Optimized Object Notation for Robot Mode","description":"Integrate TOON (toon_rust) to reduce token usage in robot mode output by 40-60%.

## Project Location
/dp/toon_rust (Rust library + CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

Robot mode outputs JSON for every command (~60 commands). This JSON is:
- Read by AI agents (tokens cost money)
- Transmitted over network (bandwidth)
- Stored in logs (storage)

JSON is verbose with repeated keys, quotes, whitespace.

## What TOON Does

TOON is a token-efficient serialization format:
- 40-60% fewer tokens than equivalent JSON
- Designed specifically for LLM consumption
- Preserves type information
- Human-readable (unlike binary formats)
- Round-trip compatible with JSON

### Example
JSON: {"panes":[{"name":"agent-1","program":"claude-code","active":true}]}
TOON: panes:[{name:agent-1,program:claude-code,active:T}]

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### Option A: Output Format Flag (Recommended)
Add --robot-output-format flag: json (default) or toon (new)

### Option B: Config-Based Default
Set default output format in config: [robot] output_format = "toon"

### Option C: Auto-Detect
If agent supports TOON, use TOON automatically.

## Implementation Plan
1. Add toon_rust as Go dependency (via CGO/FFI or subprocess)
2. Create serialization wrapper that supports both JSON and TOON
3. Add --robot-output-format flag to all robot commands
4. Add config option for default format
5. Update documentation

- Backwards compatibility: existing scripts expect JSON

- `--no-cache`, `--no-questions`
- `--project` (override working dir)

- Modifiers are **unprefixed** per robot-api-design.md.

 {"id":"bd-77bxi","title":"TOON: Research Go integration approach (CGO vs subprocess)","description":"Evaluate approaches for calling toon_rust from Go code.

## Options

### Option A: Subprocess (Simpler)
Call toon CLI as subprocess:
```go
cmd := exec.Command("toon", "encode")
cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(jsonData)
toonOutput, _ := cmd.Output()
```

Pros: Simple, no CGO, easy updates
Cons: Process overhead per call, latency

### Option B: CGO + Rust FFI (Faster)
Build toon_rust as C-compatible library, call from Go via CGO.

Pros: Near-zero overhead, native speed
Cons: CGO complexity, cross-platform builds, version coupling

### Option C: WASM (Middle Ground)
Compile toon_rust to WASM, run via Go WASM runtime.

Pros: Sandboxed, cross-platform, single binary
Cons: WASM overhead, runtime dependency

## Recommendation

Start with **subprocess** for simplicity. If latency becomes issue, migrate to CGO.

Robot mode commands are not high-frequency (maybe 1-10/second), so subprocess overhead (~5ms) is acceptable.

## Research Tasks
1. Verify toon CLI exists and has encode/decode commands
2. Benchmark subprocess overhead
3. Evaluate if CGO is needed for any use case
4. Check if toon_rust has WASM build

## Deliverable

### Scenario 2: Rate Limited Agent
1. Spawn session with agent
2. Inject rate limit message into pane (tmux send-keys simulation)
3. Call --robot-status
4. Verify:
   - rate_limit_detected is true
   - rate_limit_match contains expected pattern

### Scenario 3: Stuck Agent
1. Spawn session with agent
2. Wait >5 minutes (or mock time)
3. Call --robot-status
4. Verify:
   - seconds_since_output > 300
   - last_output_ts is stale
   - output_lines_since_last == 0

### Scenario 4: Crashed Agent
1. Spawn session with agent
2. Kill agent process directly
3. Call --robot-status
4. Verify:
   - child_pid is null or process_state is Z
   - Appropriate status indicated

### Scenario 5: Large Swarm Performance
1. Spawn session with 16 agents
2. Call --robot-status
3. Verify:
   - All fields present for all panes
   - Response time < 2 seconds
   - No race conditions

### Scenario 6: Output Activity Tracking
1. Spawn session
2. Call --robot-status, record output_lines_since_last
3. Wait for agent to produce output
4. Call --robot-status again
5. Verify output_lines_since_last increased
